0.title: Command Reference
0.content: Run wukong --help for the most up-to-date options.

1.title: Global Options
1.content: | Option | Description | . | -h, --help | Show help | . | -V, --version | Show version | . | -p, --prompt <prompt> | Run one prompt non-interactively | . | -c, --continue | Continue the previous session | . | -S, --session [id] | Resume a session | . | --yes, --yolo | Automatically approve all actions | . | --plan | Start in plan mode | .

2.title: Core Commands
2.content: wukong . Launch the interactive TUI. wukong . wukong -p <prompt> . Run a single prompt and print the response. wukong -p "explain the project structure" . wukong provider . Manage LLM providers. wukong provider . wukong login . Authenticate via the device-code flow. wukong login .

3.title: Verification Commands
3.content: wukong verify . Run deterministic checks on the current workspace and write an evidence report. wukong verify wukong verify --build wukong verify --command "pnpm test" wukong verify --json wukong verify --no-report . wukong scan . Read-only risk scan of git changes. wukong scan wukong scan --json wukong scan --report ./reports/risk.md . wukong guard . Inspect or run the command risk guard. wukong guard --status wukong guard --stats wukong guard --enable wukong guard --disable wukong guard -- rm -rf ./tmp .

4.title: Server & Web Commands
4.content: wukong server . Start the local REST/WebSocket server. wukong server run --foreground . wukong web . Open the local web UI. wukong web .

5.title: Utility Commands
5.content: wukong doctor . Validate configuration files. wukong doctor . wukong export [sessionId] . Export a session as a ZIP file. wukong export wukong export <session-id> . wukong vis [sessionId] . Open the session visualizer. wukong vis wukong vis <session-id> . wukong migrate . Migrate legacy Wukong data. wukong migrate . wukong upgrade . Upgrade to the latest version. wukong upgrade .

6.title: Exit Codes
6.content: | Code | Meaning | . | 0 | Success | . | 1 | Command failure or high-risk verification | . | 2 | Invalid arguments or environment error | .

8.title: Configuration
8.content: Wukong Code stores configuration and data under ~/.wukong. ~/.wukong/ config.toml tui.toml credentials/ .

9.title: Environment Variables
9.content: | Variable | Description | . | WUKONG_HOME | Override the default data directory | . | WUKONG_CODE_PROVIDER_NAME | Default provider name | . | WUKONG_CODE_CUSTOM_HEADERS | Custom outbound LLM request headers | . Example . WUKONG_HOME=/tmp/wukong-test wukong doctor .

10.title: Configure Providers
10.content: Use the interactive provider command: . wukong provider . Or edit ~/.wukong/config.toml directly.

11.title: Configuration Priority
11.content: Settings are resolved in order — later sources override earlier ones. | Built-in defaults | Config files in WUKONG_HOME | Environment variables | Command-line options | .

13.title: Requirements
13.content: . | macOS, Linux, or Windows | A local workspace directory | An API key for your LLM provider | . Wukong Code supports multiple providers — managed auth is optional. Bring your own keys.

14.title: Install & Verify
14.content: curl -fsSL https://wukong.today/install.sh | sh . Install on macOS or Linux: . curl -fsSL https://wukong.today/install.sh | sh . For Windows, download the .exe from the releases page. wukong --version . Verify the installation: . wukong --version .

15.title: Core Workflow
15.content: wukong provider . Configure an LLM provider: . wukong provider . wukong . Launch the interactive TUI: . wukong . wukong -p <prompt> . Run a one-off prompt: . wukong -p "summarize this repository" .

16.title: Verification
16.content: wukong verify . Run checks and write an evidence report: . wukong verify . wukong scan . Scan git changes for delivery risks: . wukong scan . wukong guard . Inspect risky commands before execution: . wukong guard --status .

17.title: Next Steps
17.content: . | Browse the command reference | Learn about configuration options | .

18.title: Wukong Code
18.content: Terminal AI coding agent . Launch an interactive TUI or run one-off prompts — without leaving your shell. Quick Install View on GitHub .

19.title: Why Wukong Code?
19.content: Terminal-native — Stay in your flow. No browser, no IDE plugin, no context switch. Verification built-in — wukong verify checks your changes, wukong scan catches delivery risks before they ship. Command guard — wukong guard inspects risky commands before execution, so AI-generated shell scripts don’t surprise you. Multi-provider — Bring your own keys. Supports DeepSeek, Moonshot, OpenAI-compatible providers, and more. Web UI & Server — wukong web opens a local UI; wukong server exposes REST and WebSocket endpoints.

20.title: Quick Install
20.content: curl -fsSL https://wukong.today/install.sh | sh . macOS, Linux, and Windows are fully supported. See Getting Started for details.

21.title: Quick Start
21.content: # Start the interactive TUI wukong # Run a one-off prompt wukong -p "explain this repo" # Verify changes before committing wukong verify # Scan for delivery risks wukong scan .
